title: Use path.isAbsolute() for platform-aware absolute path detection
context: git rev-parse --git-path hooks returns relative paths in normal checkouts but absolute paths in linked worktrees. The original code checked dir.startsWith("/") to distinguish them, but this only recognizes POSIX paths. On Windows worktrees (C:\... or C:/...), the check misfired and join(root, dir) produced malformed doubled paths like <root>\C:\…\.git\hooks where hooks were written where git would never run them. Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file).
decision: Replace string-based path detection (dir.startsWith("/")) with Node.js standard library path.isAbsolute(dir), which correctly identifies absolute paths on both POSIX and Windows platforms.
consequences: Hooks now install to the correct absolute paths on Windows worktrees
Relative path handling in normal checkouts is unchanged
POSIX worktrees continue to work as before
Code delegates platform logic to standard library instead of custom string matching
alternatives_rejected: Enhanced string matching (e.g., startsWith("/") || /^[A-Z]:/) — more brittle, less maintainable
Custom Windows drive letter detection — duplicates logic that Node already provides
Platform-specific conditional logic — unnecessary when a single API handles both
